meta.tagline: The clearest sip the mountains can give

home.heroTitle:
The clearest sip
the mountains can give
home.heroSubtitle: Suamsu is premium mineral water drawn from a volcanic rock aquifer in an isolated, pollution-free watershed in Yeosu, Jeollanam-do. We bottle nature's quiet work, unchanged.
home.sectionAboutBody: From volcanic bedrock in Yeosu, Jeollanam-do, to a global premium water brand — built carefully, one source at a time.
home.sectionProductBody: Water filtered through roughly 400m of volcanic bedrock is bottled as it is, with almost no human touch along the way.
home.sectionDistributionBody: Anchored in domestic premium channels, we are expanding step by step into Middle Eastern and Southeast Asian markets.

about.title: A promise in every sip
about.lead: Suamsu (秀岩水) means "water rising from a remarkable rock." Our first responsibility, as a company, is to protect the purity and safety of the water drawn from a volcanic rock aquifer in Yeosu, Jeollanam-do.
about.missionBody: Deliver water that is as close to nature as possible, in the most honest way possible. We choose verified water quality over loud marketing, and recyclable packaging over decorative excess.
about.visionBody: By 2030, Suamsu will be the trusted standard for Korean premium water across 10+ markets in Asia and the Middle East.

values.title: Nature, untouched
values.body: We manage the source's catchment area ourselves and continuously monitor the surrounding environment.

values.title: Transparent quality
values.body: Every well is tested against all 48 items of Korea's drinking water standards, and the test reports are open for anyone to review.

values.title: Sustainable distribution
values.body: Premium glass bottles, recyclable aluminium cans, and short-haul logistics — we lower the footprint of every single bottle.

history.body: Secured a 28,644㎡ source site in Gajang-ri, Yulchon-myeon, Yeosu, and began initial geological and hydrological surveys.
Obtained preliminary approval for spring water well development (Jeollanam-do Water Resources Division, May 2025 – Apr 2027).
Conducted environmental impact assessment and detailed water quality analysis — passed all 48 items of the drinking water standard.
Target completion of production facility and start of commercial production.
Phased expansion from a domestic launch into premium Asian and Middle Eastern markets.

about.siteBody: Suamsu begins on a 28,644㎡ site in Gajang-ri, Yulchon-myeon, Yeosu, set within an isolated watershed free of pollution sources.

product.title: Where this water comes from
product.lead: Our source lies in Yulchon-myeon, Yeosu, Jeollanam-do, inside a protected watershed with no industrial facilities. Rainwater seeps through volcanic bedrock to roughly 400 metres below the surface — nature works slowly here.
product.sourceBody: Yeosu's annual rainfall of 1,503mm exceeds the national average, feeding a stable volcanic aquifer with an 11.3% recharge rate. We draw from a fully protected zone with no industrial activity within a 5km radius.
product.qualityNotes: * Results from a Eurofins Korea test report on samples collected in January 2026. The water passed all 48 items of Korea's drinking water standards.

quality.label: pH
quality.note: Slightly alkaline, smooth on the palate

quality.label: Uranium
quality.note: Standard: 0.03 mg/L or less, trace levels

product.processTitle: From source to bottle in 4 steps

process.title: Natural filtration
process.body: Rainwater seeps through layers of volcanic basalt to a depth of roughly 400m, naturally filtered over decades.

process.title: Deep-well intake
process.body: A closed pipe system draws water directly from wells 106–110m deep, with no exposure to surface contaminants.

process.title: Minimal processing
process.body: We apply only the lightest filtration and sterilisation — never disturbing the mineral balance.

process.title: Bottled at source
process.body: Filling happens on-site at the Yeosu source, preserving the water at its freshest.

product.catchmentBody: The catchment surrounding the source is an isolated watershed cut off from outside pollution, allowing rainwater to recharge steadily and keep both quantity and quality stable.

distribution.title: From Korea to the world
distribution.lead: Suamsu is built around a phased distribution strategy — one that turns "well-made Korean volcanic mineral water" into a global category standard.

phases.title: Domestic launch foundation
phases.body: Secure stable initial volume through OEM/ODM partnerships with major retailers, while introducing our own premium brand through online channels.

phases.title: Domestic hospitality expansion
phases.body: Expand into premium hotels, fine dining, and airline lounges across Korea.

phases.title: Middle East entry
phases.body: Pursue Halal and other local quality certifications, and prepare partnerships with premium and hospitality channels in the UAE and across the Middle East.

phases.title: Global expansion
phases.body: Pursue gradual entry into the premium mineral water category in Asia and Europe.

distribution.partnersBody: Premium hotels, fine-dining restaurants, airlines, and trusted local distributors. Partnership enquiries are always welcome.
distribution.logisticsBody: Our location near Yeosu's industrial port shortens the route from source to shipping. Premium glass bottles and recyclable aluminium cans steadily reduce the environmental footprint of every bottle.

contact.title: Let's start a conversation
contact.lead: Distribution partnerships, hospitality programmes, media enquiries — all welcome. We reply within two business days.
contact.addressValue: 8 Gajang-ri, Yulchon-myeon, Yeosu, Jeollanam-do, Republic of Korea (source site)
contact.formNote: * This form is for demo purposes; submissions are not connected to a backend.

footer.tagline: The clearest sip the mountains can give.
